page: Amazon Web Services c8g.2xlarge is an ARM64-based compute-optimized server powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs operating at 2.8 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core topology. The processor is equipped with 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. It is configured with 16.0 GB of memory, yielding a compute-focused ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance includes no local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals strong performance in memory bandwidth for large blocks at 188.91 GB/sec and single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.22 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25 percent. Geekbench single-core performance at 1883.0 and text processing at 2344.0 are also strong. However, the server exhibits a notable performance tradeoff in cryptography and larger model processing. The Passmark CPU encryption test scored in the bottom 25 percent at 1509.87, and LLM inference prompt processing for a medium 7B model was poor, placing in the bottom 10 percent at 15.58 tokens per second. Other workloads, such as Redis SET operations at 18.14 million operations per second and static web serving at 19.82 GB/sec, perform in the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8g.2xlarge leverages the custom ARM64 architecture to deliver a highly resource-dense compute profile without the overhead of hyperthreading. This makes it a viable option for compute-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or high-performance encryption. Recommended workloads include single-threaded data decompression, software compilation, text processing, and lightweight web serving. It is less suited for heavy cryptographic operations or medium-to-large language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8g.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 16.0 GB of memory, 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPU. Benchmark results show strong single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core scoring 1883.0 and large-block memory read bandwidth reaching 188.91 GB/sec. However, it presents tradeoffs, including weak encryption performance and poor prompt processing for medium LLM models. This server is highly cost-efficient for dedicated-core ARM workloads, making it well-suited for software compilation, text processing, and lightweight web serving, while being less optimal for cryptographic tasks or intensive AI inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server with 8 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 16 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps network speed, offering strong single-core and text processing performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps baseline bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated-core ARM64 compute server delivering strong single-thread performance and memory bandwidth for non-cryptographic, compute-focused workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 processor at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of memory and 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Strong single-core Geekbench and large-block memory read performance
Tradeoffs include weak encryption speeds and poor medium LLM prompt processing
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for software compilation, text processing, and lightweight web serving
